action: Cycle 4 (predicate quality) — promote mutation gate WARN→fail at kill_rate<0.7; flip EVOLVE_TEST_PHASE_ENABLED default to 1
operator_note: Layer 4 (activation) of the four-layer predicate-quality defense. Scope: (1) MODIFY scripts/lifecycle/phase-gate.sh — change gate_discover_to_build mutation check from WARN-only to fail-gate at kill_rate < 0.7 (per CLAUDE.md documented promotion path). (2) MODIFY CLAUDE.md env-var table — flip EVOLVE_TEST_PHASE_ENABLED default 0 → 1; update Rule 7 to reference the four-layer defense. (3) MODIFY agents/evolve-orchestrator.md — phase flow always includes TDD between Triage and Build. (4) MODIFY docs/architecture/acs-predicate-quality-gate.md — reflect activated state. HARD GATE: a 5-cycle batch with the four-layer defense ON must show — (a) 0 grep-only predicates shipped, (b) mutation kill_rate ≥ 0.7 for all shipped predicates, (c) ≥1 cycle where predicate-quality lint catches a real attempted regression (deliberately seed one), (d) audit pass-rate unchanged vs pre-defense baseline (no false positives blocking real fixes). VERIFICATION DRILLS (run during this cycle): (i) drift drill — TDD writes behavioral predicate, operator breaks it to grep-only, gate_test_to_build MUST block; (ii) real-regression drill — Builder ships fix that breaks an existing regression-suite predicate, regression suite catches it; (iii) cost-benefit — per-cycle cost delta must be ≤ +$4.50 (linter ~$0.05 + TDD-on-Opus ~$3-5 + Auditor extension ~$0.50); (iv) false-positive drill — legitimate documentation-only fix produces [no-test-needed] marker, linter does not erroneously fail. Predecessors: Cycle 1 (author separation) + Cycle 2 (linter) + Cycle 3 (Auditor review). Plan: see evidence_pointer.
